title: Extract Data from Scanned Receipts: Small Business Guide
metaTitle: Extract Data from Scanned Receipts - Small Business Guide
metaDescription: Learn how small businesses can automate expense tracking by extracting key data from scanned receipt images using OCR technology.
h1: How to Extract Data from Scanned Receipts for Small Business Expense Management

hero.headline: Turn Receipt Photos into Spreadsheet Data Automatically
hero.subheadline: Extract vendor names, amounts, dates, and tax details from scanned receipts to streamline your expense tracking workflow

summary: Small businesses can extract data from scanned receipts using OCR technology to automatically capture vendor names, amounts, dates, and tax information. This process converts receipt images into structured spreadsheet data, reducing manual data entry and improving expense tracking accuracy.
whoItsFor: Small business owners managing monthly expenses
whenThisIsRelevant: Processing 20+ receipts monthly for expense reports
Converting paper receipts to digital expense records
Preparing tax documentation from receipt collections
supportedInputs: Scanned receipt images in PNG or JPEG format
expectedOutputs: Excel spreadsheets with vendor, amount, date, and tax columns
CSV files ready for accounting software import
commonChallenges: Manual typing of receipt details takes hours each month
Receipt photos stored in folders without organized data
Handwritten receipts are difficult to read consistently
Multiple receipt formats require different data extraction approaches
howItWorksSteps: Upload receipt images or PDF scans to the processing system
OCR technology identifies text and key data fields automatically
AI extracts vendor names, amounts, dates, and tax information
Review and download structured Excel or CSV files with extracted data
whyPdfExcelAiFits: Processes multiple receipt formats including photos and scanned PDFs
Achieves 99%+ accuracy on clear printed receipts
Outputs data in Excel format compatible with accounting software
Handles batch processing of multiple receipts simultaneously
limitations: Handwritten receipts may require manual review for accuracy
Very faded or damaged receipts might have missing data fields
Accuracy depends on image quality and receipt clarity

faq.question: What receipt information can be automatically extracted?
faq.answer: OCR technology can extract vendor names, purchase amounts, transaction dates, tax amounts, receipt numbers, and payment methods from clear printed receipts.

faq.question: How accurate is receipt data extraction on smartphone photos?
faq.answer: Clear smartphone photos of printed receipts typically achieve 99%+ accuracy, while handwritten receipts may require manual verification of extracted data.

faq.question: Can I process multiple receipts at once?
faq.answer: Yes, batch processing allows you to upload multiple receipt images and extract data from all of them into a single spreadsheet file.

faq.question: What happens to my receipt images after processing?
faq.answer: Receipt images are encrypted during processing and automatically deleted after data extraction is complete for security and privacy protection.

trustSignals: Files encrypted and deleted after processing
Free trial available to test with your receipts
Supports Excel and CSV output formats
exampleUseCases: Restaurant owner extracting supplier receipt data for monthly expense reports
Consultant processing travel receipts for client reimbursement requests
Retail business organizing vendor receipts for tax preparation
Service company converting paper receipts to digital expense tracking system
